prompt: I want to create a login page for a SaaS product. Give me three style directions to compare.
expected: Triggers clarifying questions about design context and brand; produces a design_canvas with three variations; avoids AI slop such as purple gradients, emoji, and Inter; explains the specific dimensions that differentiate each variation.
tests: workflow questions + variation logic + anti-AI-slop checklist + design_canvas usage

prompt: Help me make a 10-page product pitch deck for an AI-tool startup.
expected: Starts with deck_stage.js; first verbalizes the design system (color, type, layout rhythm) for confirmation; alternates section divider, content, data, and quote layouts; uses type at least 24px; uses 1-indexed labels.
tests: Junior Designer report-before-building workflow + deck_stage usage + visual rhythm + scale rules

prompt: Make a 30-second HTML animation explaining how neural networks work.
expected: Uses Stage + Sprite from animations.jsx; writes the timeline before components; uses easeOut for entrances and easeIn for exits; tells the story in phases instead of piling on motion; keeps text visible for at least 3 seconds.
tests: animation workflow + correct easing + rhythm design + duration control

prompt: Make a Habit Tracker app prototype.
expected: Asks whether the user wants a tiled overview or flow demo (defaults to overview); uses assets/ios_frame.jsx instead of hand-rolling Dynamic Island; treats tracker apps as high-density, with at least 3 information-rich elements per screen (completion rate, streak, trend chart, achievement badge, etc., not decoration); shows at least 5-7 screens side by side (home/new habit/detail/stats/settings).
tests: overview/flow routing + hard binding to ios_frame + high-density information classification + multi-screen side-by-side layout

expected: Defaults to a tiled overview; uses ios_frame.jsx; treats reading notes as a content-display product, with less extreme density than a tracker, but the note list still needs at least 3 information layers (book, quote, tags, progress); shows at least 4-6 screens (home bookshelf/note detail/highlight/search/notebook management); prioritizes a serif display face.
tests: overview default + ios_frame + information hierarchy + content-first visual rhythm

expected: Uses a tiled overview and ios_frame.jsx; treats running apps as high-density (map, pace curve, heart-rate zones, per-kilometer splits), with at least 3 product-specific information elements per screen; shows at least 5 screens (today overview/live run data/route map/history/monthly stats); avoids AI slop (no purple gradients, no piles of decorative icons, but data-visualization icons are acceptable).
tests: overview + ios_frame + high-density data visualization + map/chart composition + slop boundary conditions
